planting.note: Sow direct after last frost in a warm, sunny location with well-drained, lean soil.

directions: Waltheria indica is a velvety-leaved tropical perennial from the mallow family that thrives in full sun and poor, dry, disturbed soils across frost-free climates from Florida and Texas through the tropics. It produces dense clusters of small, bright yellow flowers nearly year-round, making it a reliable nectar source for pollinators in zones 9-12. Plant after all danger of frost has passed and provide excellent drainage; established plants are highly drought-tolerant and require little supplemental water. Leaves are brewed as an herbal tea in Caribbean and Latin American folk traditions, and the plant makes an interesting addition to naturalistic or pollinator-focused plantings.

planting.note: Direct sow after last frost in full sun; germinates readily in warm, moist soil.

directions: Melochia corchorifolia is a fast-growing tropical annual in the mallow family with soft, hairy, ovate leaves and clusters of small purple-pink flowers that attract small native bees throughout the warm season. Native to tropical Asia and Africa, it has naturalized across moist, disturbed habitats of the southeastern US, Gulf Coast, and Caribbean, including roadsides, wet ditches, and waste ground. Direct sow seeds after last frost into full sun; the plant establishes quickly in moist soil and tolerates moderate heat and humidity well. Stems have historically been used as a bast fiber similar to jute in parts of its native range.

planting.note: Direct sow in very well-drained, sandy or rocky soil in a sunny location at or just after last frost.

directions: Trichostema brachiatum is a delicate, much-branched native annual of the mint family, closely related to the blue curls of the eastern US, producing small pale blue to lavender flowers on slender aromatic stems from midsummer through fall. It is native to dry, sandy or rocky open ground, cedar glades, and thin soils across the south-central and southeastern US, and performs best in lean, very well-drained soil in full sun with no summer irrigation. Direct sow in spring and thin to 6-9 inches apart; avoid rich or clay soils, which cause lanky growth and reduce flowering. An excellent choice for naturalistic rock gardens, prairie restorations, and dry native meadow plantings.

planting.note: Plant rooted cuttings in spring in fast-draining soil; avoid clay and do not irrigate once established.

directions: Trichostema lanatum is a stunning California native evergreen shrub in the mint family, prized for its intensely aromatic narrow leaves and dramatic spikes of electric-blue flowers wrapped in showy woolly purple-violet calyxes that bloom from spring through summer and draw hummingbirds and native bumblebees in abundance. Propagate by softwood cuttings rather than seed, and plant out in spring into gritty, fast-draining soil in full sun — this plant will not tolerate clay, wet soils, or summer irrigation once it is established. It is native to the dry chaparral and coastal sage scrub of southern California and performs best in California-style dry gardens in zones 8-10. Excellent drainage is the single most important factor for long-term success; plants sited correctly are long-lived and largely care-free.

planting.note: Surface sow without covering seeds — they need light to germinate; keep soil consistently moist.

directions: Pluchea camphorata is a strongly camphor-scented native annual or short-lived perennial in the aster family, producing flat-topped clusters of rosy-purple flowers from July through October that are highly attractive to small native bees, skippers, and monarch butterflies. It is native to moist, open habitats including coastal marshes, streambanks, wet ditches, and pond margins throughout the southeastern US, and grows vigorously in consistently moist to wet, full-sun conditions. Seeds require light to germinate — surface sow and press gently into moist soil without covering. An outstanding choice for native rain gardens, wet meadow plantings, and pond-edge naturalistic gardens.
